00020 
00024 #include "wrappers.h"
00025 
00026 #include "class.h"
00027 #include "classfile.h"
00028 #include "field.h"
00029 #include "verifier.h"
00030 
00031 /******************************************************************************
00032  * Verifier implementation                                                    *
00033  ******************************************************************************/
00034 
00040 bool same_package(const class_t *cl1, const class_t *cl2)
00041 {
00042     uint32_t i = 0;
00043     uint32_t sl1 = 0;
00044     uint32_t sl2 = 0;
00045     const char *str1 = cl1->name;
00046     const char *str2 = cl2->name;
00047 
00048     while (str1[i] != 0) {
00049         if (str1[i] == '/') {
00050             sl1 = i;
00051         }
00052 
00053         i++;
00054     }
00055 
00056     i = 0;
00057 
00058     while (str2[i] != 0) {
00059         if (str2[i] == '/') {
00060             sl2 = i;
00061         }
00062 
00063         i++;
00064     }
00065 
00066     if (sl1 != sl2) {
00067         return false;
00068     }
00069 
00070     if ((sl1 == 0) && (sl2 == 0)) {
00071         return true;
00072     } else if (memcmp(str1, str2, sl1 + 1) == 0) {
00073         return true;
00074     }
00075 
00076     return false;
00077 } // same_package()
00078 
00088 void verify_field(const class_t *cl, const field_info_t *info,
00089                   const field_attributes_t *attr)
00090 {
00091     uint16_t access_flags = info->access_flags;
00092     const char *name = info->name;
00093     const char *descriptor = info->descriptor;
00094     field_t *field;
00095 
00096     // Check for duplicates
00097     for (size_t i = 0; i < cl->fields_n; i++) {
00098         field = cl->fields + i;
00099 
00100         if ((strcmp(field->name, name) == 0)
00101             && (strcmp(field->descriptor, descriptor) == 0))
00102         {
00103             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00104                     "Class contains a duplicate field");
00105         }
00106     }
00107 
00108     // Check if the field descriptor is sound
00109     field_parse_descriptor(descriptor);
00110 
00111     // Check if the constant value corresponds to the correct field
00112     if (attr->constant_value_found)
00113     {
00114         if (!(access_flags & ACC_STATIC)) {
00115             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00116                     "Non-static field has a ConstantValue attribute");
00117         }
00118 
00119         // A reference constant must be a string
00120         if (descriptor[0] == 'L') {
00121             if (strcmp(descriptor, "Ljava/lang/String;") != 0) {
00122                 c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00123                         "A constant static field holds a non-String reference");
00124             }
00125         }
00126     }
00127 
00128     // Check if access flags are sound
00129     if (((access_flags & ACC_PUBLIC) && (access_flags & ACC_PROTECTED))
00130         || ((access_flags & ACC_PUBLIC) && (access_flags & ACC_PRIVATE))
00131         || ((access_flags & ACC_PROTECTED) && (access_flags & ACC_PRIVATE))
00132         || ((access_flags & ACC_FINAL) && (access_flags & ACC_VOLATILE)))
00133     {
00134         c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR, "Illegal field's access flags");
00135     }
00136 
00137     // Check if access flags are compatible with the class holding this field
00138     if (class_is_interface(cl)) {
00139         if (!((access_flags & ACC_PUBLIC) && (access_flags & ACC_STATIC)
00140               && (access_flags & ACC_FINAL)))
00141         {
00142             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00143                     "Interface has non-static, public, final field");
00144         }
00145 
00146         if (access_flags & ACC_TRANSIENT) {
00147             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00148                     "Interface field has ACC_TRANSIENT access flag set");
00149         }
00150     }
00151 } // verify_field()
00152 
00160 void verify_field_access(const class_t *acl, const class_t *cl,
00161                          const field_t *field)
00162 {
00163     /* If we got here we actually found a field, let's check if we are allowed
00164      * to access it */
00165 
00166     if (field_is_private(field)) {
00167         if (acl != cl) {
00168             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00169                     "Trying to access a private field from an external class");
00170         }
00171     } else if (field_is_protected(field)) {
00172         if ((acl != cl) && !class_is_parent(cl, acl) && !same_package(cl, acl))
00173         {
00174             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00175                     "Trying to access a protected field from a non-child class "
00176                     "of a different package");
00177         }
00178     } else if (!field_is_public(field)) {
00179         if (!same_package(cl, acl)) {
00180             // Field is package visible, if ACC_PUBLIC is set no check is needed
00181             c_throw(JAVA_LANG_NOCLASSDEFFOUNDERROR,
00182                     "Trying to access a package-visible field from a different "
00183                     "package");
00184         }
00185     }
00186 } // verify_field_access()
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:27 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
